home.upcomingSubtitle: Compete across Asia. Qualify for the Grand Championship.
home.featuredDesc: The Marianas Open is the biggest and longest established jiu-jitsu organization in the Marianas, with tournaments since 2007. Our goal is to provide a professional and well-organized tournament experience for all athletes, coaches, and spectators.
home.internationalReachDesc: Partners in Korea, Japan, Taiwan, Philippines, and Hong Kong. Featured in 30,000+ printed magazines across Asia.

event.title: Guam Marianas Open 2026
event.scheduleDesc: Divisions run simultaneously across 6 mats. Black belt finals in the evening.
event.travelDesc: Guam is a visa-free destination for Japanese, Korean, and Taiwanese nationals. Direct flights available from Tokyo, Osaka, Seoul, Manila, and Taipei.
event.flightsDesc: United Airlines and regional carriers offer direct flights from major Asian cities. Flight time: 3-4 hours from most hubs.
event.hotelsDesc: Hyatt Regency Guam, Dusit Thani Guam Resort, and Holiday Resort & Spa offer competitor group rates.
event.visaDesc: Guam Visa Waiver Program applies to citizens of Japan, South Korea, Taiwan, and many other countries for stays up to 45 days.
event.step1Desc: Create your competitor profile on the Asian Sport Jiu-Jitsu Federation website.
event.step2Desc: Find 'Guam Marianas Open 2026' in the event listings and select your division.
event.step3Desc: Pay the registration fee online. Early bird discounts available.
event.shareText: Join us at Guam Marianas Open 2026 - The Ultimate International BJJ Championship in the Pacific!
event.visaEstaDesc: Japan, South Korea, Taiwan, and 30+ countries
event.visaGuamCnmiDesc: Philippines, Hong Kong, select countries (45-day stay)

about.heroSubtitle: Guam's premier martial arts tournament and international championship since 2007
about.p1: Since its inception in 2007, the Marianas Open has grown into one of the premier martial arts tournaments in the Pacific region. Hosted annually in Guam, this event has emerged as a symbol of excellence, uniting athletes, families, and supporters from around the world.
about.p2: The Marianas Open is more than just a competition—it's a celebration of community, culture, and the spirit of martial arts. Over the years, it has expanded into an international series, with tournaments held in Japan, the Philippines, Korea, and Guam, culminating in the grand finale on our home island. This event not only showcases the best in jiu-jitsu talent but also fosters a supportive environment for our youth and their families, promoting discipline, respect, and unity.
about.p3: Every year, the Marianas Open attracts participants of all ages and skill levels, from novice competitors to seasoned champions. Our commitment to excellence is reflected in the quality of competition and the sense of camaraderie that defines the event. In addition to the tournament this year, we will host an awards ceremony to honor our athletes' achievements and recognize the invaluable support of our sponsors.
about.p4: This event will be covered by international media and live-streamed by KUAM News, allowing people from around the world to tune in. With extensive coverage from international press, the Marianas Open connects with a global audience, giving attendees a chance to be part of an event that resonates deeply with our community values.
about.p5: Join us as we continue to build on this legacy, making each year's Marianas Open better than the last. Together, we can support the next generation of martial artists and strengthen the bonds that unite us.
about.timeline2007: The Marianas Open is founded in Guam as a local jiu-jitsu tournament.
about.timeline2015: The tournament expands with international competitors from Japan and the Philippines.
about.timeline2019: Pro Series launches with events across Asia—Japan, Philippines, Korea.
about.timeline2025: 1,300+ competitors from 6 countries. $2M economic impact to Guam.
about.timeline2026: International Pro Series spans 7 events across 6 countries.
about.contactDesc: Questions about the Marianas Open? Reach out to our team.

terms.title: Terms and Conditions
terms.liability: I hereby assume full responsibility and liability for all damages, injuries, and losses that I may sustain or incur while participating in or watching the tournament.
terms.injury: I realize that serious injury is common place in martial arts tournaments such as the one I am hereby entering, and that death is a possibility. I also realize that if I am injured, I might be disfigured, disabled and/or rendered unable to work again. By entering into this competition, I agree to and fully accept and assume liability for any and all injuries that I may incur. I expressly waive any and all claims that I or anyone acting on my behalf or through me might have against Fury Promotions, the Marianas Open, Copa de Marianas, event promoters, operators, sponsors, officials, participants, non-participants, or volunteers for any injury regardless of its nature, effect or affect on me as a result of my participation and/or presence at the tournament.
terms.medical: I authorize, and agree to hold harmless, any medical personnel that may be present at the event to take any action necessary, should I become injured.
terms.media: I understand that tickets will be sold to spectators to watch the tournament; that news media and others might cover the tournament and that it may also be recorded or otherwise shown to audiences around the world. I hereby expressly and irrevocably forever consent to have my likeness shown, publicized, commented and/or reported on; and I do not expect to, and in fact waive any compensation I might otherwise be entitled to as a result thereof.
terms.health: I represent that I am in good health; that I am not presently, nor will I participate while under the influence of any drug or medication; that no one affiliated with this tournament has encouraged me to enter or made any representations regarding my fitness or ability to participate; that I have read and understood every provision of this release; and that I am legally competent to and freely enter into this waiver, release and assumption of risk agreement.
terms.organizer: Organizer: Fury Promotions, LLC/Marianas Open/Marianas Pro (hereinafter 'organizer') expressly reserves the right, in its sole and absolute discretion, with or without cause, to change, modify or otherwise edit information contained upon the registration form, including but not limited to: the competitor's academy name and affiliation and/or the competitor's name and/or nickname.
terms.changes: Changes to a competitor's registration (including but not limited to: academy affiliation) will be permitted at an announced date. There will be no changes permitted after that time.
terms.headCoach: The head coach of an academy's competition team shall be granted the right to change a competitor's registration information. I further understand that I am responsible for checking my registration information for weight, belt, academy, age division, and name information at an announced date.
terms.verification: I understand that if I don't verify my information and send a notification for correction by the specific time, no changes will be permitted after that time and any mistakes in the brackets, whether or not caused by me, may lead to my immediate disqualification.
terms.denial: Organizer hereby expressly reserves the right, in its sole and absolute discretion, with or without cause, to deny a potential competitor's registration for permission to compete in the event. The terms athlete and/or competitor may be used interchangeably.
terms.dispute: If any dispute arises out of, or is any way related to your participation in this event, by entering your registration application, you are hereby expressly waiving and giving up your right to the filing of a lawsuit in the city, state/country or federal court and a trial by jury.
terms.minor: If the competitor is a minor I, hereby certify that I am the parent and/or legal guardian of the named minor who has been given my express permission to compete in the event for which we are registering.
terms.organizerNotice: Fury Promotions, LLC/Marianas Open/Marianas Pro. All rights reserved.

rankings.title: Rankings
rankings.subtitle: ASJJF ranking points awarded at Marianas Open Pro Series events.
rankings.note: Points are awarded based on the star rating of each event. 'Alone' points are awarded when a competitor wins a division with no opponents. Team points follow the 4-Star scale for all events.
rankings.dynamicSubtitle: Live rankings calculated from official ASJJF event results across all Marianas Open tournaments.
rankings.formula: Fighter (5-Star): Gold 75, Silver 35, Bronze 15. Team (4-Star): Gold 21, Silver 9, Bronze 3.
rankings.noResults: No results found for these filters.
rankings.formulaNote: Official reference: Fighter ranking points use 5-Star values; team ranking points use 4-Star values. Alone brackets and same-team one brackets follow organizer policy on the official rankings page.

rules.title: Competition Rules
rules.subtitle: A clearer overview of the standards competitors should expect at Marianas Open events.
rules.intro: This page summarizes the rule areas competitors most often need before weigh-ins, bracket checks, and match day.
rules.officialNote: Full official rulebook hosted by the Asian Sport Jiu-Jitsu Federation at

general.title: General Competition Rules
general.desc: Match format, scoring, submission rules, and referee decisions

uniform.title: Gi & Uniform Requirements
uniform.desc: Approved gi specifications, patches, and no-gi attire standards

prohibited.title: Prohibited Techniques
prohibited.desc: Techniques not permitted by belt rank and age division

points.title: Points & Advantages
points.desc: How points, advantages, and penalties are scored

time.title: Match Time Limits
time.desc: Official match durations by belt rank and age group

competitionFormat.title: Competition format
competitionFormat.description: How matches are structured, officiated, and decided.
items: Matches follow federation-style brackets, referee commands, and division rules.
Age group, belt rank, and match procedures can change what is allowed in a division.
Organizer announcements, posted brackets, and mat-side instructions still matter on event day.

giNoGi.title: Gi and no-gi uniforms
giNoGi.description: What athletes should wear and what can cause a uniform check failure.
items: Uniforms must be clean, competition-ready, and free of tears or unsafe alterations.
No-gi gear must fit properly, and shorts should not include exposed zippers or unsafe pockets.
If gear does not meet inspection standards, competitors may need to change before competing.

pointsAdvantages.title: Points and advantages
pointsAdvantages.description: How positional scoring, advantages, and penalties affect a result.
items: Scoring follows the rulebook for positional control, takedowns, sweeps, passes, and dominant finishes.
Advantages and penalties are used when points do not fully separate competitors.
Referee judgment and table decisions should be treated as the official result on the mat.

illegalTechniques.title: Illegal techniques and safety
illegalTechniques.description: Safety expectations, foul categories, and disqualification risks.
items: Illegal moves vary by belt rank and age division, so athletes should review the full rulebook before competing.
Dangerous behavior, unsportsmanlike conduct, and prohibited submissions can lead to penalties or disqualification.
Basic hygiene, trimmed nails, and safe equipment remain mandatory for all competitors.

matchTimes.title: Match times and divisions
matchTimes.description: What influences bout length and divisional structure.
items: Match duration depends on division, age, and belt level.
Gi and no-gi divisions may have different operational details even when the event weekend is shared.
Athletes should verify their bracket, schedule, and weigh-in instructions before the event starts.

rules.opensExternally: Opens the downloadable rules PDF in a new tab.
rules.note: Use the summaries below to prepare, then reference the official ASJJF rules source and supporting SJJIF rulebook resources. If an organizer bulletin, bracket note, or official announcement gives event-specific instructions, follow those instructions on event day.
rules.rulebookCopy: Download a PDF copy of the SJJIF rulebook used as a supporting reference alongside ASJJF guidance.
rules.eventDaySubtitle: The operational details below are just as important as the technical rules.
rules.fullRulebookSubtitle: ASJJF is the primary official rules reference for this event series. SJJIF materials remain available as the underlying rulebook resource.
rules.footerNote: This local guide is meant to improve clarity and avoid dead-end links. It does not replace official ASJJF guidance, SJJIF rulebook language, or event-specific organizer instructions.
rules.maintenanceSubtitle: This guide is maintained locally so competitors are not sent to unreliable third-party rules pages.
rules.maintenanceBody: If federation language, organizer procedures, or event-day requirements change, this page should be updated to match the latest approved guidance.
rules.contactSubtitle: For event-specific clarifications, bracket issues, or organizer instructions, contact the Marianas Open team directly.
rules.contactNote: Use the federation rules source or downloadable PDF for full rule language, but use the organizer contact above when you need answers specific to this event series.
eventDayChecklist: Bring a valid government photo ID.
Check your bracket, schedule, and correction deadline before event day.
Confirm your registered weight in kilograms and be ready for event-day weigh-in procedures.
Arrive early because matches can move ahead of schedule.
Wear sandals or shoes off the mat and bring a backup uniform if possible.
